name: Weird Checks
blurb: Strange dares and stranger opportunities. Roll when a player attempts something odd enough to deserve its own fate, or when the world offers a peculiar challenge.

1.text: Hold a perfectly straight face while the settlement's worst liar tells their tale. Instinct, hard.
1.reward: The liar becomes your informant for a season — they respect nothing so much as an unreadable face.

2.text: Out-stare the old ram that has claimed the wood-pile. Presence, moderate — it has been practicing longer than you.
2.reward: The ram follows you thereafter at a dignified distance. Once, when it matters, it will headbutt someone you truly need headbutted.

3.text: Thread a fishhook by moonlight with cold-numbed fingers while someone tells you urgent news. Finesse, hard.
3.reward: Your hands remember it forever: +1 to your next three Finesse rolls made under pressure.

4.text: Recite the names of all fifty settlers, in the order they stepped off the ship. Knowledge, moderate.
4.reward: The old ones weep. Gain 2 Hope, and the settlement's elders will thereafter answer one hard question a season honestly.

5.text: Carry a brimming bowl of stew up the watchtower ladder without spilling. Agility, moderate; hard if it's windy, and it's always windy.
5.reward: Calder shares the stew and one true thing from his list of what moves in the dark.

6.text: Split a log with one blow in front of everyone after loudly announcing you will. Strength, hard.
6.reward: A work-song is invented about you on the spot. Once this year, invoke it to give the whole party advantage on a group action.

7.text: Sleep a full night in the storehouse rafters without Ellory noticing. Agility then Presence, both moderate.
7.reward: You now know the storehouse's other inventory — the drawer, the manifest, the things counted that aren't food.

8.text: Convince a crow to trade you what it's carrying. Presence, hard; offering something shiny shifts it to moderate.
8.reward: The crow's trinket is always worth more than what you gave. Always. The crows have decided they like doing business with you.

9.text: Walk the palisade's top beam end to end at dusk, arms out, no hands. Agility, hard.
9.reward: The children of the settlement believe you can do anything. Once, that belief will be mechanically true: turn one failure witnessed by a child into a success.

10.text: Eat whatever Jory cooked without your face saying anything at all. Instinct, very hard.
10.reward: Jory quietly starts asking you first before he 'improves' anything. This is worth more than gold.

11.text: Mend a stranger's boot so well they don't notice it was ever torn. Finesse, moderate.
11.reward: The stranger, whoever they turn out to be, owes you one small kindness — and strangers here are never only strangers.

12.text: Stay awake through Lyra's entire silent dawn watch beside her, without speaking once. Instinct, hard.
12.reward: She teaches you to read the ridge line. Advantage on your next three wilderness Instinct rolls.

13.text: Make bread rise in winter in a cold kitchen through sheer attention and love. Knowledge, hard.
13.reward: +1 Morale to the stores, and the recipe becomes yours — the settlement calls the loaf by your name.

14.text: Beat Garrick at arm wrestling. Strength, very hard. He will not let you win, out of respect.
14.reward: Win or lose, gain a Hope. Win, and Garrick tells you one thing he has never told anyone — GM's choice of thread.

15.text: Catch the storehouse cat, who has never been caught. Agility, very hard.
15.reward: You may not keep the cat. But the cat keeps you: once per session it appears somewhere useful — atop the thing you're looking for.

16.text: Read the whole sky: name tomorrow's weather, loudly, and be right. Instinct, hard.
16.reward: The settlement plans around your word now. Once a season, declare the weather narratively convenient and it is so.

17.text: Dance the old-country harvest dance correctly, all eleven turns, with everyone watching. Presence, hard.
17.reward: For one evening the settlement forgets it is fifty people in the dark. +2 Morale, and everyone clears a Stress.

18.text: Whittle something true with your off hand while telling the story of who it's for. Finesse and Presence, both moderate.
18.reward: The carving is a small anchor: whoever carries it may reroll one Fear-heavy roll while it's on them. One carving at a time exists.

19.text: Follow the fox that watches the bunkhouse, all the way to where it goes. Agility and Instinct, both hard.
19.reward: Where the fox goes is worth knowing — the GM places one revealed secret of the nearby hexes, a season early.

20.text: At full dark, ring the watchtower bell exactly once so softly that only one person in the settlement hears it — the person who needed to wake. Finesse, nearly impossible.
20.reward: The bell remembers your hand. Once in the campaign, ring it from anywhere: one person of your choosing, anywhere in the world, wakes and knows the settlement needs them.
